title: Live Hash & Tamper Detector
description: Compute and compare cryptographic hashes of digital evidence, entirely in the browser and offline. Type evidence text and the tool computes its MD5 (128-bit) and SHA-256 (256-bit) hex digests live. A 'Tamper: flip one character' button mutates a single character and re-hashes, highlighting every changed hex digit and counting them so the avalanche effect is visible at a glance. A separate 'Compare two files' section hashes two text blocks with SHA-256 and reports a color-coded MATCH (bit-for-bit identical original) or MISMATCH (tampered) with the count of differing digits. SHA-256 uses the Web Crypto API; MD5 uses a compact public-domain implementation, so nothing is sent over the network.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Digital Forensics
Cryptographic Hashing
MD5
SHA-256
Evidence Integrity
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — compute and compare MD5 and SHA-256 digests and demonstrate the avalanche effect from a single-character change.

educational.topic: Using cryptographic hash functions to verify the integrity of digital evidence and detect tampering
learningObjectives: Compute the MD5 and SHA-256 hex digests of a block of evidence text.
Demonstrate the avalanche effect: change one character and observe roughly half the digest change.
Compare two files by SHA-256 to decide whether they are bit-for-bit identical.
Use hash matching to authenticate an untouched original and exclude tampered copies.
educational.prerequisites: Familiarity with the idea of a hash as a fixed-length fingerprint of data and that identical input yields an identical hash

pedagogical.interactionPattern: A clean light panel built from ordinary DOM elements (no canvas). An 'Evidence text' textarea is prefilled with a sample sentence; as the investigator types or clicks 'Compute Hashes', the MD5 and SHA-256 hex digests update live in monospace. A 'Tamper: flip one character' button mutates exactly one character, re-hashes, highlights every hex digit that changed, and reports the counts (the avalanche effect). A second 'Compare two files' section takes two text blocks, hashes both with SHA-256, and shows a green MATCH or a red MISMATCH with the number of differing hex digits and both digests, the differing digits highlighted. Everything runs offline in a secure context.
pedagogical.assessment: Application with immediate feedback: the student watches the same input always yield the same digest, then sees a one-character edit rewrite nearly the whole fingerprint, reinforcing that a hash either matches exactly or it does not. The compare section turns this into an authentication task — given two files, the student uses the SHA-256 match/mismatch to decide which is the untouched original.
pedagogical.modelType: Deterministic cryptographic model: MD5 (128-bit) via a public-domain implementation and SHA-256 (256-bit) via crypto.subtle.digest. The avalanche count is the Hamming distance in hex digits between the pre-edit and post-edit digests; the compare verdict is exact string equality of two SHA-256 digests.
controls: 'Evidence text' textarea — the input whose hashes are computed live
'Compute Hashes' button — recompute the MD5 and SHA-256 digests
'Tamper: flip one character' button — mutate one character, re-hash, and highlight the avalanche of changed digits
'File A' and 'File B' textareas — the two inputs to compare
'Compare' button — hash both files with SHA-256 and report MATCH or MISMATCH
